beats.narration: Every certification you hold traces back to a document that is not a law. SNT TC One A is a recommended practice.

payload.title: SNT-TC-1A
payload.sub: A recommended practice — not a law.

beats.narration: ASNT publishes it as guidelines for employers who certify their own people. Its verb is should, not shall. Alone, it obligates no one.
It gains force in two steps. A contract or code invokes it. Then the employer writes a controlled written practice. Auditors grade you against that.

chips.t: A contract or code invokes it

beats.narration: The written practice also draws the ladder. A trainee works only under supervision. Level One stays inside the instruction — interpretation begins at Level Two.
So hold the distinction. The book recommends; your written practice is what your program lives and dies by.

payload.sub: Documented and invoked — that is the requirement you're audited against.
